title: Articles and noun gender
objectives: Recognize common gender patterns and key exceptions
grammar_notes: Default pattern: -o = masculine, -a = feminine. Plenty of exceptions though.
Spanish has 2 genders, Russian has 3 — don't blindly import Russian gender intuitions.
Greek-origin -ma nouns (problema, sistema, tema, drama) are masculine.
Feminine nouns starting with stressed 'a' / 'ha' take 'el' (el agua, el alma) — but adjectives still agree as feminine: el agua fría.
example_phrases: El libro es interesante. (The book is interesting.)
Quiero una manzana. (I want an apple.)
El agua está fría. (The water is cold.)
